---
title: "create_order"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `create_order()`

Create Order

## Usage

```r
create_order(
  ticker,
  side,
  action,
  count,
  type = "limit",
  yes_price = NULL,
  no_price = NULL,
  expiration_ts = NULL,
  sell_position_floor = NULL,
  buy_max_cost = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `ticker` | Character. The market ticker. |
| `side` | Character. Order side: "yes" or "no". |
| `action` | Character. Order action: "buy" or "sell". |
| `count` | Integer. Number of contracts. |
| `type` | Character. Order type: "limit" or "market". Defaults to "limit". |
| `yes_price` | Integer. Price in cents (1-99) for yes contracts. Required for limit orders. |
| `no_price` | Integer. Price in cents (1-99) for no contracts. Alternative to yes_price. |
| `expiration_ts` | Integer. Unix timestamp for order expiration. Optional. |
| `sell_position_floor` | Integer. Minimum position to maintain when selling. Optional. |
| `buy_max_cost` | Integer. Maximum cost in cents for buy orders. Optional. |
| `kalshi_access_token` | Character. Environment variable name for API key. |
| `demo` | Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE. |

## Value

A list with order details including order_id.

## Description

Creates a new order in a market. Orders can be limit or market orders,
for either yes or no contracts.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
# Buy 10 yes contracts at 45 cents
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  yes_price = 45
)

# Sell 5 no contracts at 60 cents
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54",
  side = "no",
  action = "sell",
  count = 5,
  no_price = 60
)

## End(Not run)
```

## See also

Other trading: 
`amend_order()`,
`batch_cancel_orders()`,
`batch_create_orders()`,
`cancel_all_orders()`,
`cancel_order()`,
`decrease_order()`,
`get_order()`
